valid.name: with-set
valid.code:
CREATE FUNCTION fn() RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = pg_catalog, pg_temp
AS $$ BEGIN END $$;

-- SECURITY INVOKER (the default) is fine.
CREATE FUNCTION fn2() RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY INVOKER
AS $$ BEGIN END $$;

-- No SECURITY DEFINER → out of scope.
CREATE FUNCTION fn3() RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$ BEGIN END $$;

invalid.name: missing-set
invalid.code:
CREATE FUNCTION fn() RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
AS $$ BEGIN END $$;

errors.message: `SECURITY DEFINER` function must `SET search_path = ...` (e.g. `pg_catalog, pg_temp`) so an attacker-controlled schema in the caller's `search_path` cannot shadow built-in objects called from inside the function body.
